data.title: Blue Hat completes $13.3M in gold trades in H1, projects 550kg for full year
data.description:
Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ:BHAT) announced that it completed over 123KG ($13,326,754) in gold trading during the first half of 2025, and projects that trading volume may potentially reach 550 kg during fiscal year 2025.

"This represents progress in the company's strategic transformation toward gold-focused commodity trading," Chen Xiaodong, CEO of the company, said.

data.title: Bitdeer reports 33% rise in self-mined bitcoins in August
data.description:
• Bitdeer Technologies (NASDAQ:BTDR) self-mined 375 bitcoins in August, an increase of ~33% from the past month due to higher average self-mining hashrate from energization of SEALMINERs.
  • Self-mining hashrate rose by 35% to 30.0 EH/s on continued deployment of SEALMINERs. "Our operational team continues to execute and we are well on our way to achieving our ambitious target of 40 EH/s of self-mining by the end of October 2025.” said Matt Kong, Chief Business Officer at Bitdeer.
  • Total hash rate under management as of August 31, 2025 across the company’s primary business lines were 44.2 EH/s vs. 36.4 EH/s at the end of July.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China’s Xpeng recalls 70% of P7+ EVs over steering defect
data.description:
• Chinese EV maker Xpeng (NYSE:XPEV) is recalling 47,490 P7+ sedans, affecting over 70% of owners, due to a steering defect that presents a safety risk.
  • Some of the cars, produced between Aug. 20 last year and April 27 may see the steering fault warning indicator light come on and then experience a steering failure due to a flaw in the sensor wiring harness, a statement released by China’s State Administration for Market Regulation on Friday said.
  • Zhaoqing Xiaopeng New Energy Investment Co. will replace the improved steering gear assembly for vehicles within the recall scope free of charge to eliminate safety hazards.

data.title: European indexes mixed while investors read Fed rate cut clues
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.15 am ET.

London (UKX) +0.41% to 9,335, marking a second straight session of gains, driven largely by strength in miners. UK's GDP stalled in July. Manufacturing production in the UK dropped 1.3% M/M in July. UK construction output rose 2.4% Y/Y in July.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.16% to 23,663. Germany's inflation rises to 5-month high of 2.2% in August.

France (CAC:IND) -0.46% to 7,787. France's inflation rate decreased to 0.9% in August.

Spain's annual inflation rate remains unchanged at 2.7% in August.

Industrial production in Hungary dropped to 1% Y/Y in July. Hungary’s construction output rose by 4.9% Y/Y in July.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.06% lower to 555, marking a fifth straight session of gains as investors bet on a Federal Reserve rate cut next week. Meanwhile, the ECB signaled its rate-cutting cycle is over, with President Lagarde saying the bank is in a “good place” and that growth risks are now more balanced. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points to 4.05%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 2.69%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 4.65%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Camtek prices upsized $425M convertible notes offering due 2030
data.description:
Camtek (NASDAQ:CAMT) Friday announced the pricing of $425 million aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.

The offering was upsized from the previously announced $400 million aggregate principal amount of notes.

Camtek has granted to the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase, for settlement within a 13-day period beginning on, and including, the date on which the notes are first issued, up to an additional $75 million aggregate principal amount of the notes.

The offering is expected to close on September 16, 2025.

data.title: Bank of America to redeem all €2B floating rate senior notes
data.description:
• Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) plans to redeem all €2B principal amount outstanding of its Floating Rate Senior Notes, due September 22, 2026.
  • The notes were issued under the Bank of America Corporation's $65B Euro Medium-Term Note Program.
  • The redemption price will be equal to the optional redemption amount of €1,000 per €1,000 calculation amount, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the redemption date of September 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Emperor Metals announces private placement increase to $10 million
data.description:
• Emperor Metals (OTCQB:EMAUF) on Friday announced that it has increased the size of its "best efforts" private placement to up to $10M.
  • The increased offering consists of up to 15M units of the corporation at a price of $0.20 per common unit for gross proceeds of up to $3M and up to 25M units issued on a "flow-through" basis at a price of $0.28 per FT unit for gross proceeds of up to $7M.
  • Each common unit will be comprised of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant shall be exercisable to acquire one common share at a price of $0.35 per common share for a period of 24 months from the closing of the offering.
  • Each FT unit will be comprised of one common share issued on a "flow-through" basis and one-half of one warrant.
  • Closing of the offering is expected on or about October 7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: The Fresh Factory secures $4 million credit facility
data.description:
• The Fresh Factory (TSXV:FRSH:CA) on Friday said it has secured a three-year revolving asset-based lending facility in the principal amount of $4 million with Mitsubishi HC Capital America, an arm's length party to the Company.
  • Under the terms of the facility, up to $4 million (about C$5.5 million) will be made available to the company's U.S. operating subsidiaries, secured against the company's unencumbered assets.
  • Funds drawn on the facility will bear interest at prime plus 1.00% per annum, payable monthly.
  • Proceeds are expected to be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release
